reasoning_content:
I see that the test case with #include has a lot of tokens from the included file. Let me look at a simpler test case to understand the format better. Let me check what tokens are needed based on the test cases I've seen so far.

From the test cases, I need to support:
1. Keywords: int, return, if, else, void, while, break, continue, const
2. Operators: +, -, *, /, %, =, ==, !=, <, >, <=, >=, &&, ||, !, ++, --, +=, -=, *=, /=, %=
3. Punctuation: (, ), [, ], {, }, ;, ,, .
4. Identifiers
5. Numeric constants (decimal, octal, hexadecimal)
6. Comments (should be skipped)
7. Preprocessor directives (line markers)

Let me now build the current task1 and see what score I get, then I'll improve it.

tool_call.arguments: {"command": "cd /workspace/YatCC && cmake --build build -t task1 2>&1 | tail -20", "summary": "Build task1 target"}
